methods.description: This method involves the calculation of multiple sequence alignments (MSAs) and the reconstruction of phylogenetic trees using various computational tools to understand evolutionary relationships.

steps.description: Multiple sequence alignments (MSAs) were calculated for the full-length P. tricornutum proteins listed in supplementary table S1, Supplementary Material online (MSA1.fa), for the full-length protein set of TPT coorthologs from 41 annotated genomes (MSA2.fa) and for a subset of them (MSA3.fa) with MAFFT E-INS-i version 7.037b (Katoh and Standley 2013) with default settings.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate MSAs using MAFFT for protein sequences

steps.description: The individual MSA was visualized and manually curated using Jalview version 2.8.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize and curate MSA using Jalview

steps.description: For all curated MSAs the LG + G4 + I + F model was selected as the best fitting amino acid substitution model according to Bayesian Information Criterion in ProtTest version 3.3.
steps.searchable_summary: select best amino acid substitution model using ProtTest

steps.description: To reconstruct the phylogeny we used the Neighbor-Joining (NJ) method implemented in MEGA6, Bayesian phylogenetic inference implemented in MrBayes version 3.2.4 and the maximum-likelihood (ML) method implemented in RAxML version 8.1.15 and IQ-TREE version 1.34.
steps.searchable_summary: reconstruct phylogeny using NJ, Bayesian, and ML methods

steps.description: For the NJ method, an optimal tree was initially computed using the JTT matrix-based method by modeling rate variation among sites with a gamma distribution and a shape parameter of one removing all ambiguous positions in a pairwise fashion. A bootstrap consensus tree was inferred by 1,000 replicates.
steps.searchable_summary: compute NJ tree and bootstrap consensus

steps.description: For MrBayes, two runs of four Markov-chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) chains of 2 × 10^7 generations each from a random starting tree, sampling every 1,000 generations were initiated. A 25% burn-in was chosen and convergence was assessed by standard deviation of split frequencies falling below 0.01.
steps.searchable_summary: run MCMC chains in MrBayes and assess convergence

steps.description: For RAxML, initially the best ML tree among 20 generated trees with the best likelihoods was determined using the PROTGAMMAILGF model. Next, we conducted 1,000 non-parametric bootstrap inferences with the rapid hill-climbing mode.
steps.searchable_summary: determine best ML tree and perform bootstrap in RAxML

steps.description: For IQ-TREE, a ML tree was generated and the branch support values were assessed with the ultrafast bootstrap approximation and with single branch tests using 1,000 replicates each.
steps.searchable_summary: generate ML tree and assess branch support in IQ-TREE

steps.description: Phylogenetic trees were rooted either by midpoint or by specific sequences and visualized using Figtree version 1.4.2 and Adobe Illustrator CS5.

methods.description: This method involves the identification and analysis of putative TPT genes in the genome of P. tricornutum using bioinformatic tools.

steps.description: Nine putative TPT genes were identified in the genome of the diatom P. tricornutum by BLAST searches using the T. gondii TgAPT as an initial query.
steps.searchable_summary: identify TPT genes using BLAST search

steps.description: Additional genes encoding TPT-family domains were detected via hmmer searches and were grouped by subsequent phylogenetic analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: detect TPT-family domains using hmmer and group by phylogenetic analysis

steps.description: Bioinformatic analyses revealed the presence of six to ten potential TMDs for all TPTs and clear prediction of putative N-terminal targeting signals for specific TPTs.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze TMDs and predict N-terminal targeting signals

methods.method_name: Analysis of Target Membranes and Orientations of the TPTs
methods.description: This method uses a self-assembling GFP system to determine the localization and orientation of TPT proteins in P. tricornutum.

steps.description: To determine whether TPT4a and TPT4b are IEM-or OEMproteins, we took advantage of the self-assembling GFP system.
steps.searchable_summary: use self-assembling GFP system to determine protein localization

steps.description: A fusion protein consisting of the full-length sequence of TPT1 and the small self-assembling GFP fragment GFP_S11 was simultaneously expressed with a cytosolically localized large GFP_S1-10 fragment.
steps.searchable_summary: express fusion protein with GFP fragments to test localization

steps.description: To prove the localization and orientation of the two plastidal TPTs, either TPT4a or TPT4b was expressed with a C-terminal GFP_S1-10 fusion together with a plastid stroma targeted GFP_S11.
steps.searchable_summary: express TPTs with GFP fusions to determine localization and orientation
